teamObjective.explanation: Player 0 is the leading opponent with only 2 cards left (public opponent_near_finish tag). By playing a legal beat I take the lead away from the near-finish opponent and deny them an easy path to exit.

partnerBelief.summary: Partner has 3 cards remaining and no public signal suggests they are near finishing; they may be able to support later if needed.

opponentBelief.summary: Player 0 appears near finishing with only 2 cards left and has just led a low heart single; they may be trying to shed a small card or set up a quick exit.

actionRationale.primaryReason: Player 0 led a low heart 3 and is down to 2 cards, so I should beat it with my lowest heart 4 to take the lead, suppress the near-finish opponent, and keep my higher hearts (9, 10) for later control.

whyNotAlternatives.reason: Passing would let the near-finish opponent retain the lead and likely finish soon; I have a cheap legal beat, so I contest the trick.
The 9 also wins the trick but is a stronger card than necessary; the 4 already beats the lead and preserves middle strength.
The 10 wins as well, but it is my highest heart and I want to save it for a future critical trick rather than spend it now.

risks: Beating with the 4 still leaves player 0 with 2 cards; they might win back the lead with a higher heart or a trump.
If my partner cannot support, my remaining 9 and 10 may not be enough to stop a fast opponent finish.
riskAssessment.mitigation: By winning this trick cheaply I gain the next lead and can choose a card that forces the opponent to spend a higher resource before they can exit.
